texto: Fear's a box we grow used to, convince ourselves it's all the space we need, that we like its color, its smell, its protection. Comes a time to stop hiding, stop being afraid. If we don't break free of our boxes, our spirits' shrink, we shrink in every way imaginable. Oh, Grace, my friend, don't let fear, especially someone else's fear, prevent you from living your life.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Joan Medlicott: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Fear's a box we grow used to, convince ourselves it's all the space we need, that we like its col…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, fear, shrink pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e reflexao.
